title: Proto-Persona
definition: A hypothesis-driven persona created before research to align the team on assumed user types.
example: Before interviews, the team drafts 'Ops Olivia' — manages 3 tools daily, hates context-switching — then validates or discards traits after 6 user calls.
whenToUse: Sprint zero or when you need quick alignment before interviews.

title: Behavioral Segmentation
definition: Grouping users by what they do, not who they are demographically.
example: You segment by 'runs payroll weekly' vs 'runs payroll monthly' instead of by company size — because behavior predicts needs better than demographics.
whenToUse: Prioritizing which user segment to serve first.
interviewPrompts: How often do you do X?
What triggers you to use Y?

title: Anti-Persona
definition: A user type you explicitly choose not to serve, preventing feature creep.
example: You document 'enterprise IT buyer' as anti-persona for your self-serve SMB tool — so when sales asks for SSO-only enterprise features, you have a clear scope boundary.
whenToUse: When stakeholders push features for edge users.
officeUse: Scope management and saying no with evidence.
